Title: Chloroplast ATP-dependent metalloprotease FtsH5/VAR1 confers cold-stress tolerance through singlet oxygen and salicylic acid signaling

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Plant defense hormone / phenolic signal metabolite
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense/stress signaling; overaccumulation impairs chloroplast development and growth under cold
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SA degradation by NahG partially/strongly suppresses var1 cold-sensitive chlorosis and growth retardation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in var1 background; phenotyping under cold stress
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NahG overexpression reduces endogenous SA in var1. 2. This substantially restores chlorophyll content, Fv/Fm, and growth under cold stress. 3. Results support SA overaccumulation as a major cause of var1 cold sensitivity.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates proof-of-concept that lowering SA can alleviate cold-induced chlorosis, though bacterial transgene deployment is mainly a research tool rather than direct breeding strategy.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species is bacterial; used here as a functional SA-depletion tool rather than endogenous plant pathway gene.

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress (12°C day/8°C night; also short-term 5°C transfer in some assays)
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Salicylic acid biosynthesis via the chloroplast isochorismate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to isochorismate in chloroplasts for SA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Plant defense hormone / phenolic signal metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedling aerial tissues/chloroplast-associated SA biosynthesis context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and stress responses; excessive accumulation under cold impairs chloroplast development and growth
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Specifically upregulated in var1-1 under cold stress; strongly repressed in ex2 var1
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point and major SA biosynthetic enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation promotes SA overaccumulation in cold-stressed var1; ics1 mutation partially suppresses var1 chlorosis and growth retardation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; SA quantification; genetic knockout in ics1 var1 double mutant; phenotyping under cold stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS1 was specifically induced in cold-stressed var1-1. 2. This induction correlated with ~17-fold higher free SA accumulation. 3. Mutation of ICS1 partially rescued chlorosis, chlorophyll loss, low Fv/Fm, and growth retardation of var1 under cold stress.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing excessive ICS1 activity could mitigate maladaptive SA overaccumulation under prolonged cold stress, but complete suppression may compromise defense-related stress signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper concludes SA in cold-stressed var1 is synthesized primarily through ICS rather than PAL pathway.

Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress (12°C day/8°C night; mild cold), with some short-term 5°C assays
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in hetero-hexameric thylakoid FtsH complex with type A/B FtsHs including FtsH1, VAR2/FtsH2, and FtsH8
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Photosystem biogenesis/repair and indirect control of singlet oxygen and salicylic acid signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Proteolytic control of chloroplast protein homeostasis; loss of function triggers plastid retrograde signaling and SA overaccumulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cold stress induces VAR1 transcript accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Photosystem subunits including PsbA/D1 and other PSI/PSII components; indirectly affects SORGs, SARGs, ICS1, EDS1, PAD4, and PhANGs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Functional VAR1 suppresses inappropriate activation of SARGs/SA biosynthesis genes and maintains photosynthesis-related gene/protein accumulation under cold stress
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Plant defense hormone / phenolic signal metabolite
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress and defense signaling; excessive accumulation is detrimental to chloroplast development and growth under cold
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major determinant preventing cold-induced SA overaccumulation by maintaining chloroplast proteostasis and limiting singlet oxygen retrograde signaling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by cold stress; most highly upregulated among thylakoid FtsHs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of VAR1 causes cold hypersensitivity, chlorosis, reduced chlorophyll and PSII efficiency, elevated singlet oxygen, and strong SA overaccumulation/signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (var1-1); native-promoter complementation; RNA-seq; proteomics; RT-qPCR; SA measurement; SOSG singlet oxygen staining; genetic interaction analyses
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VAR1 is essential for cold-stress tolerance in Arabidopsis. 2. var1-1 accumulates singlet oxygen and strongly activates SA biosynthesis/signaling under cold stress. 3. Complementation restores cold tolerance, whereas SA depletion or ex1/ex2 mutations suppress the var1 phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing VAR1 function could improve chloroplast resilience and prevent maladaptive SA/ROS signaling under low-temperature cultivation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes severe chlorosis, impaired chloroplast biogenesis, growth retardation, and photosynthetic defects under cold
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a transcription factor, VAR1 is the central experimentally tested gene controlling nutrient-related metabolite signaling (SA) indirectly via chloroplast status.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts with PAD4 as direct partner
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: SA biosynthesis and SA-responsive defense signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation pathway promoting ICS1 expression and sustaining SARG expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Acts downstream of EX1-mediated singlet oxygen signaling in var1 under cold stress
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of ICS1 and downstream SA signaling genes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Plant defense hormone / phenolic signal metabolite
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong positive regulator of SA accumulation and SA-dependent transcriptional reprogramming in cold-stressed var1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Elevated in var1-1 under cold stress; mildly repressed in ex1 var1 and strongly repressed in ex2 var1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: eds1 mutation strongly suppresses var1 cold-sensitive chlorosis, growth retardation, reduced chlorophyll, and low Fv/Fm
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic double/triple mutants; RT-qPCR; SA quantification; phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EDS1 expression is elevated in cold-stressed var1-1. 2. eds1 strongly suppresses the var1 phenotype more than ics1. 3. EX1 acts upstream of EDS1, whereas EX2 shows epistasis over EDS1 in this context.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Fine-tuning EDS1 may reduce maladaptive SA overactivation under cold stress, but caution is needed due to its broad defense roles.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EDS1 contributes to both SA-dependent and SA-independent defense gene expression, explaining stronger rescue than ics1.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts with EDS1 as partner module
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Promotes SA accumulation and activates SA-related defense programs
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Singlet oxygen/retrograde signaling context in var1 under cold stress
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Plant defense hormone / phenolic signal metabolite
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of SA accumulation contributing strongly to var1 cold-sensitive phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Elevated in cold-stressed var1-1; strongly repressed in ex2 var1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: pad4 mutation strongly suppresses chlorosis and growth defects of var1 under cold stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic double/triple mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAD4 is induced in var1-1 under cold stress. 2. pad4 mutation strongly alleviates the var1 cold-sensitive phenotype. 3. EX2 is epistatic to PAD4 in the analyzed genetic background.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: PAD4 is a potential node to dampen excessive cold-induced SA signaling, but manipulation may compromise immunity.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes stronger rescue by pad4/eds1 than by ics1 due to broader transcriptional control.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Singlet oxygen-triggered plastid retrograde signaling linked to SA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Mediates 1O2-dependent retrograde signaling upstream of EDS1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Singlet oxygen accumulation in cold-stressed var1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of a subset of SARGs/SORGs, but only weakly in var1 compared with EX2
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Plant defense hormone / phenolic signal metabolite
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent upstream mediator linking chloroplast singlet oxygen to EDS1-dependent SA signaling in var1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function inferred genetically; ex1 mutation only mildly represses SARG induction in var1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ex1 mutation partially suppresses var1 chlorosis under cold stress, but less strongly than ex2 and without reducing SA as much
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic double/triple mutants; SOSG staining context; transcriptomics; RT-qPCR; SA measurement
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EX1-mediated singlet oxygen signaling acts upstream of EDS1 in var1 under cold stress. 2. ex1 partially suppresses cold sensitivity of var1. 3. EX1 contributes only weakly to SA reduction compared with EX2 in this system.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulation of EX1 alone may provide only limited improvement in maladaptive cold-induced SA signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Role differs from the classic flu system, where EX1 is more dominant.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Singlet oxygen-triggered retrograde signaling, SA biosynthesis/signaling, and repression of photosynthesis-associated nuclear genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Promotes SARG expression and represses PhANG expression; partly independent of EX1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Singlet oxygen accumulation in cold-stressed var1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ICS1; EDS1; PAD4; CBP60G; WRKY28; WRKY41; SARGs; PhANGs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SA biosynthesis/signaling genes and represses photosynthesis-associated nuclear genes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Plant defense hormone / phenolic signal metabolite
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Critical regulator in this study; stronger than EX1 for promoting SA-related transcription and cold-sensitive phenotype in var1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function revealed genetically and transcriptomically; ex2 mutation strongly represses ICS1/EDS1/PAD4 and SARG expression in var1 background
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ex2 mutation significantly suppresses var1 chlorosis, improves chlorophyll and PSII efficiency, reduces SA accumulation, and derepresses PhANGs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic double/triple mutants; RNA-seq clustering; RT-qPCR; SA quantification; phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EX2 is a major determinant of cold-sensitive var1 phenotypes. 2. ex2 suppresses SA biosynthesis/signaling genes and lowers free SA more strongly than ex1. 3. EX2 additionally represses PhANG expression, indicating an EX1-independent role.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: EX2 is a promising node to uncouple maladaptive singlet oxygen/SA signaling from chloroplast growth repression under cold stress, but broader stress-response consequences need evaluation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No additive rescue with ex1 ex2 var1; EX2 is epistatic over ICS1/EDS1/PAD4 in this context.
